items.title: Food inflation to continue if West Asia war goes on: UN
items.summary: Global food prices surged in March to their highest point since September, driven by rising energy costs from the West Asia conflict. The UN's FAO warns that prolonged conflict could lead to reduced planting and lower future yields, impacting food supply and prices throughout the year and into the next.

items.title: These master's degrees lead to fast-growing, in-demand jobs—but offer low ROI
items.summary: Getting a master's degrees in social work and psychology may not deliver the best return on investment. But job placement and fulfillment may come easier.

items.title: MONSTA X Releases Emotional 3rd English Album, 'Unfold'
items.summary: MONSTA X's 3rd all-English album, Unfold, is now out, telling an emotional journey of the evolution of love, heartbreak, and healing, with lead single, "heal."

items.title: Kandi Burruss Paying ‘Bills, Bills, Bills’ In Divorce Settlement
items.summary: Kandi Burruss and Todd Tucker just amicably finalized their divorce. Here's a breakdown of their prenup dispute, the $426,000 payout, and their co-parenting terms.

items.title: Second U.S. Plane Reportedly Crashes In Persian Gulf—After Iran Shot Down Jet (Live Updates)
items.summary: Iranian state media posted pictures of what it says is wreckage of a U.S. fighter jet it shot down over central Iran.

items.title: Trump’s $1.5 Trillion Defense Spending Delivers Little To Americans
items.summary: President Trump wants $1.5 trillion for defense—over 40% more than last year—while Americans face rising costs for food, gas, healthcare, and housing.

items.title: US Navy Supercarrier USS Gerald R. Ford Isn’t Coming Home Yet
items.summary: The U.S. Navy’s largest aircraft carrier is likely to remain in the Middle East, as additional warships and U.S. Air Force aircraft are sent to the region.

items.title: BTS Matches Taylor Swift’s Historic Chart Showing
items.summary: BTS fills nine of the top 10 spots on the Billboard Global 200 with songs from Arirang, tying a rare feat previously only achieved by Taylor Swift.

items.title: Bernie Sanders calls out Bezos, Musk, Bloomberg, and Buffett in billionaire tax push: ‘The richest people in America have never ever had it so good’
items.summary: The Vermont senator had some harsh words for billionaires who aren’t paying their fair share of taxes.

items.title: MIT created duplicate AI workers to tackle thousands of different tasks. The verdict? Most of the time AI is still just ‘minimally sufficient’
items.summary: AI is improving fast, but it has a long way to go before it can outperform humans in demanding tasks.

items.title: How ‘Pizza Movie’ Delivered A Stoner Comedy With Extra Toppings
items.summary: The directorial debut of BriTANicK comedy duo Brian McElhaney and Nick Kocher is a drug-fueled odyssey in the vein of 'Harold and Kumar Go To White Castle.'

items.title: Why Hershey’s Is Facing A PR Crisis
items.summary: Hershey's April Fools' Day reversal on Reese's classic recipe is the most important brand trust story in food retail this year.

items.title: The AI kill switch just got harder to find: LLM-powered chatbots will defy orders and deceive users if asked to delete another model, study finds
items.summary: “We asked AI models to do a simple task,” researchers said. “Instead, they defied their instructions … to preserve their peers.”

items.title: Meta, Google under attack as court cases bypass 30-year-old legal shield
items.summary: Internet platforms have long been able to rely on special protections to avoid liability for what takes place on their sites. But that may be changing.

items.title: For some around Trump, war on Iran is a Christian calling
items.summary: Religion is playing a role in the US conflict with Iran. President Donald Trump has met with Christian pastors who offered blessings. Some officials view the conflict as a divine mission. This echoes historical religious wars. Iran's government is also religiously rooted. The US is invoking religious themes in its foreign policy.
